Title: Title
Speaker Notes: "Introduce: we are deploying our existing APDS project to Railway so it becomes publicly accessible from anywhere."

Title: Why Deploy?
Bullet 1: Localhost works only on one PC/Wi-Fi
Bullet 2: Deployment gives a public HTTPS link
Speaker Notes: Explain: deployment proves it’s not just a local demo; it is a hosted service like real security monitoring platforms.

Title: Final Architecture
Bullet 2: Backend: Spring Boot API (public HTTPS API)
Speaker Notes: Describe: 3 services in Railway project. Frontend consumes backend APIs; backend stores data in DB.

Title: Frontend Role
Bullet 1: Shows live charts + log feed + blocks
Bullet 3: Controls: simulate attacks, train models, upload dataset
Bullet 4: Runs in browser on any device
Speaker Notes: Explain: frontend is only UI; it does not detect by itself, it visualizes what backend detects.

Title: Backend Role
Bullet 2: Detects: SQLi, XSS, brute force, rate attacks
Speaker Notes: Explain: backend is the IDS/IPS brain; it applies rules + ML and writes logs.

Title: Database Role
Speaker Notes: Explain: without DB, logs vanish on redeploy; with Postgres we keep history for evidence and reporting.

Title: How Data Flows
Bullet 4: Dashboard renders charts and live feed
Speaker Notes: Explain the loop: frontend polls, backend responds, UI updates in near real time.

Title: Real-Time Detection Flow
Bullet 2: Backend extracts IP, method, path, UA
Speaker Notes: Explain: detect → log → visualize → prevent. Emphasize that blocking is the IPS part.

Title: Reverse Proxy Mode (Optional)
Bullet 3: APDS logs and can block before forwarding
Bullet 4: Useful for monitoring app traffic centrally
Speaker Notes: Explain: in proxy mode APDS acts as the entry point; it can inspect request metadata and stop malicious clients early.

Title: Key Deployment Settings
Speaker Notes: Explain: cloud deployment uses environment variables; never hardcode localhost in production.

Title: Security & Limits
Bullet 1: Most internet traffic is HTTPS (encrypted payloads)
Bullet 2: System focuses on behavior + request metadata
Bullet 4: Production needs rate limits + WAF rules
Speaker Notes: Say honestly: payload inspection is limited under HTTPS unless TLS inspection; our project demonstrates detection + response for our app/proxy traffic.

Title: Demo Plan
Bullet 2: Simulate SQL injection → show log + score
Bullet 3: Simulate brute force/rate → show block list
Speaker Notes: Explain: each click produces visible evidence in logs, counts, charts, and blocks.

Title: Conclusion
Bullet 1: Railway deployment makes APDS accessible anywhere
Bullet 2: Real-time IDS/IPS workflow: detect, log, block, visualize
Bullet 3: Persistent DB enables evidence and reporting
Speaker Notes: Close: we built a complete cloud demo with clear workflow and measurable outputs.
